channel.title: CTF on Hexomedine
channel.description: Recent content in CTF on Hexomedine

item.title: [Root-Me 10k CTF] Misc - White Rabbit
item.description: The challenge In this challenge, we had to exploit a discord bot. In fact, there was an OS command injection in the bot. Here’s the description : Write-Up The purpose of the bot is to check if a root-me challenge domain is up. So we can DM the bot and see what’s what. The normal behavior of the bot is represented below. As I’ve done this type of challenges before, this challenge screams “OS command injection” to me.

item.title: [Root-Me 10k CTF] OSINT - Find Our Roots
item.description: The challenge The OSINT challenge was to find the name and the call sign of the boat on the picture below. Here’s the description of the challenge : Write-Up exif data ? So the first thing I’ve done is to check the image with exiftool to see if there’s nothing helpful. We can see that the picture has a creator, “Rene Te Pas” and a creation date. On to google I reverse search the image on google and yandex but I found nothing that useful.

item.title: [Root-Me 10k CTF] Web - CVE Finder Engine
item.description: The challenge In this challenge, we were tasked to retrieve the admin’s credentials. This was done by exploiting a CQL injection. Here’s the description : Write-Up Intro This is a basic application in which it is possible to look for some CVEs. Going through Burp, we can notice that two POST parameter are sent : query, which is the parameter we search for, and fields. The “fields” parameters are in fact the fields of the database the finder engine will return to us.
